description: Live-DB proof that DatabaseOutboxRepository.claimNextAtomic (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED) never double-claims or drops a distribution_outbox row under real concurrent load. No production code change — scoped proof-only lane per the issue's plan-gate spec.

sha_binding.verified_source_note: Proof-only lane — no production source changed; verified_source_sha binds to the commit introducing the test file itself.

runtime_proof.note: Live-DB concurrent-claim proof run against real Supabase (project zfzdnfwdarxucxtaojxm). Creates real picks + distribution_outbox rows, then races claimNextAtomic concurrently.
queries: INSERT submissions + picks + distribution_outbox (enqueue) x8, one row per synthetic pick, same target
RPC claim_next_outbox (claimNextAtomic, SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED) x12 called concurrently via Promise.all against the same target

row_counts.note: 8 rows enqueued for one synthetic target (utv2-1497-canary-<runId>), all 8 claimed exactly once across 12 concurrent claimNextAtomic callers; 4 excess callers received null.

receipts.note: Live Supabase run. No production code touched — proof-only lane confirming existing claim_next_outbox RPC is race-safe.

acceptance_criteria.n_concurrent_claims_against_real_rows: PASS — 12 concurrent claimNextAtomic callers against 8 real Postgres rows for one target
acceptance_criteria.no_row_claimed_twice: PASS — claimed-id set size equals row count (8), no duplicates
acceptance_criteria.no_row_dropped: PASS — claimed set exactly equals enqueued set
acceptance_criteria.no_production_code_change_unless_race_su: PASS — zero races surfaced; claim_next_outbox / claimNextAtomic untouched
